title: Healthy Eating Habits
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Making good (0) nutritional choices is one of the most important things a person can do for their health. A (17) balanced diet rich in fruit, vegetables, and whole grains can reduce the risk of many (18) preventable diseases. However, busy modern lifestyles often make it (19) difficult for people to prepare fresh, home-cooked meals every day.||The food industry also plays a (20) significant role, as (21) heavily processed foods are often marketed as (22) convenient alternatives to healthy options. Public health campaigns aim to raise (23) awareness of the benefits of nutritious eating, particularly among young people. Introducing better food education in schools could make a real (24) difference to the nation's long-term health.

example.text: Making good (0) NUTRITIONAL choices is one of the most important things a person can do for their health. (NUTRITION)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'balanced' is formed from 'balance' by adding the suffix '-d'.
The adjective 'preventable' is formed from 'prevent' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'difficult' is the base form; 'difficulty' is the noun formed by adding '-y'.
The adjective 'significant' is formed from 'signify' by replacing '-ify' with '-icant'.
The adverb 'heavily' is formed from 'heavy' by changing '-y' to '-ily'.
The adjective 'convenient' is formed from 'convenience' by replacing '-nce' with '-nt'.
The noun 'awareness' is formed from 'aware' by adding the suffix '-ness'.
The noun 'difference' is formed from 'differ' by adding the suffix '-ence'.
